verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, whose characterized members (IZUMO1, IZUMO3, IZUMO5) are sperm-specific proteins with roles in fertilization. However, the NCBI localization data explicitly assigns IZUMO4 to the nucleus, and no documented evidence of surface accessibility, membrane anchoring, or cell-state-induced ectodomain display is established for this protein. The nuclear localization is the defining compartment; no contextual surface-display pathway (cancer ecto-presentation, developmental germline-restricted display, cell-death induction, or activation-linked exocytosis) has been documented to override this baseline.
